Notice Of Motion And Motion For Limited Lift Of Stay For Purpose Of Moving To Sever Claim Against Hopkins & Carley
Off Calendar May-01-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PAUL J. STEINER, Esq. of Law Offices of Paul J. Steiner (Tel: 415-981-6100) for plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HOPKINS & CARLEY, A LAW CORPORATION's Motion For Limited Lift Of Stay For Purpose Of Moving To Sever Claim Against Hopkins & Carley.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The "motion for limited lift of stay for purpose of moving to sever claim against Hopkins & Carley" is denied.

Hopkins & Carley is the last of five named defendants in this case, the lion's share of which has been compelled to arbitration. Meanwhile, the claim against Hopkins & Carley is stayed.

Hopkins & Carley argues that grant of its motion "will further judicial economy." (Rply. 3:24.) However, grant would trigger, at a minimum, a motion to sever, a demurrer and adjudication of plaintiff's demand for discovery outside arbitration. This court's experience in similar cases is that, if the stay remains in effect, the claim against Hopkins & Carley is unlikely to ever be adjudicated. Thus, economy will be furthered by maintaining the stay.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS RENEE KWAN, ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC, and 350 WSJ LLC's Motion To Vacate Order Compelling Arbitration And Related Stay.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Renee Kwan, Asset Management, LLC, and 350 WSJ LLC's motion to vacate order compelling arbitration and related stay is denied.

Plaintiffs' arguments regarding enforceability of the arbitration provision have been twice rejected, and the Court declines to revisit them a third time. Plaintiffs' argument regarding the possibility of inconsistent rulings, raised for the first time in this motion, lacks merit. The only new fact plaintiffs raise is that the Court granted their fee waiver on June 6. The fee waiver is insufficient to justify vacating the order compelling arbitration and stay.

Order signed.
